hub_subtitle: Business English para seguros, reuniões e entrevistas — e o desbloqueio da fala, do primeiro dia ao último

lesson.menu_title: How Often Do You...? -- My Professional Habits
lesson.menu_desc: A aula em que o ingles para de falar da agenda dela e comeca a falar DELA. Seis palavras da caixa de entrada (email, check, send, reply, update, late) e os cinco adverbios de frequencia -- always, usually, often, sometimes, never --, cuja unica dificuldade nao e o significado, e o LUGAR: o portugues deixa o "sempre" andar pra onde quer, o ingles tem um endereco so. Fecha com o ensaio da entrevista da Zurich: "How often do you visit clients?" -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 7 -- How Often Do You...?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Paula Morales | Lesson 7 -- How Often Do You...?
lesson.grammar_point: frequency adverbs (always / usually / often / sometimes / never) -- position before the main verb and after the verb to be

listenings.text: Hi, Paula. David here. Welcome to your second week. Let me tell you how our team works — the rhythm, not the rules. We always start the day with email. I check my inbox at eight, every morning. Sofia usually sends her update on Friday, at five — she is never late, and nobody here is. We visit brokers on Thursday. Not every Thursday — sometimes twice a month, sometimes more. And the reports go out on Friday. Always. One more thing, and this is the important one: I often write in English, and my English is not perfect. I always make mistakes, and I always send the email anyway. Nobody ever answered me about my grammar. They answer about the account. See you at nine. Goodbye.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here. Two things about the rhythm of the commercial area, and then I let you work. One: I always want the update on Friday. Always. Not perfect — just on time. A short email is enough. Three lines: the account, the number, the next step. Two, and listen to this one carefully: you don't need to reply immediately. I never reply immediately. When my phone rings at seven in the evening, I don't answer it. I answer in the morning, and the world is still there. New people are often afraid of this. Don't be. In this team we are never late — but we are never in a hurry either. And one last thing, Paula. Your English. You sometimes stop in the middle of a sentence, and you think I notice. I don't. I notice that you understand every word I say. That is not a small thing — that is most of the job. See you on Friday.

items.situation: David asks: "How often do you check your email?" You check it every morning, at eight. Answer with a full sentence — not with one word.
tips: I always check my email in the morning.
I check my email at eight, every day.

items.situation: An interviewer asks: "How often do you travel for work?" You travel two or three times a year. Answer — and remember that "sometimes" alone is not an answer in an interview.
tips: Two or three times a year. Not very often.

items.situation: A broker sends you an email at eight in the evening. You don't answer at night — ever. Say it, politely, and say when you do answer.
tips: I usually reply in the morning.

items.situation: Sofia asks if you are late with the report. You are not — you are never late. Answer her. Careful: this one has the verb to be in it.
tips: The report is always ready on Friday.

items.situation: You want to know the broker's habit — how many times he comes to the Zurich office. Ask him.
tips: How often do you visit our office?
How often do you come here?

items.situation: An interviewer asks: "Do you usually work with brokers directly?" Yes — that is your job, every day. Answer with the frequency word in the right place.
tips: Yes. I usually work with brokers directly.
I work with brokers every day.

bank.label: Saying how often / Dizer com que frequencia
items: I always check my email in the morning.
I usually reply in the morning, never at night.
I often send an update to the broker.
I sometimes travel for work. Not very often.
I am never late with a deadline.

bank.label: Asking and answering / Perguntar e responder
items: How often do you send the update?
How often do you visit brokers?
Every day. / Twice a month. / Every week.
Do you usually work with brokers directly?
